title: testnet_sapling_addr_str returns a UA string but name implies raw Sapling bech32
description: testnet_sapling_addr_str() produces a Unified Address (u1...) with a Sapling receiver, not a raw ztestsapling... bech32 address. The function name and the test that uses it ('parse_address_valid_sapling_testnet_succeeds') imply a raw Sapling address. This is not wrong — parse_sapling_address handles UAs with Sapling receivers — but a contributor writing a test that specifically needs the ztestsapling... encoding (e.g., to test the raw Sapling branch of TryFromAddress) would use this helper and get the wrong format. Fix: rename to testnet_sapling_ua_str() and add a comment explaining that raw ztestsapling... encoding requires a different path (ZcashAddress exposes no public from_sapling constructor).
close_reason: renamed testnet_sapling_addr_str → testnet_sapling_ua_str; updated test docstring to document UA vs raw bech32 distinction
